title: Add directory ignore functionality
description:
Implement directory ignore functionality to exclude certain directories from vault scanning, similar to Obsidian TaskNotes plugin.

Key requirements:
- Support reading ignore patterns from Obsidian TaskNotes settings (.obsidian/plugins/tasknotes/data.json)
- Support Neovim configuration override
- Provide sensible defaults (.obsidian, .trash, .git, .claude)
- Use efficient find -prune for filesystem-level exclusion
- Invalidate cache when ignore patterns change
- Add debug command to troubleshoot ignore configuration

User reported seeing template files with placeholder titles in task list.
design:
Three-tier configuration priority system:
1. Neovim config override (config.ignore_dirs) - complete replacement when set
2. Obsidian settings (excludedFolders from data.json) - merged with defaults
3. Defaults (.obsidian, .trash, .git, .claude) - always used unless overridden

Implementation approach:
- Add DEFAULT_IGNORE_DIRS constant to config.lua
- Create get_ignore_dirs() helper with priority logic
- Build find command with -prune flags for each ignored directory
- Track ignore_dirs in cache metadata for validation
- Parse Obsidian excludedFolders (comma-separated string)
- Create :TaskNotesDebugIgnore command for troubleshooting
acceptance_criteria:
- Directory ignore patterns read from Obsidian settings
- Neovim config can override all ignore patterns
- Default directories (.obsidian, .trash, .git, .claude) excluded by default
- Cache invalidates when ignore patterns change
- find -prune used for efficient directory exclusion
- :TaskNotesDebugIgnore command shows current configuration
- Template files no longer appear in task list
- All tests pass (85 tests)
